name: Abheri
description: An audava-sampurna (asymmetric) raga with deeply devotional character.

name: Abhogi
description: Pentatonic (audava-audava) raga with a serene, contemplative mood.

name: Anandabhairavi
description: Raga of love and longing; associated with Syama Sastri's finest kritis.

name: Atana
description: A rakti raga known for vigour; many Tyagaraja kritis.

name: Bhairavi
description: One of the most popular and emotionally deep ragas in Carnatic music.

name: Bilahari
description: Bright, devotional raga often used for mangalam pieces and bhajans.

name: Devagandhari
description: Serene raga associated with devotion and grace.

name: Dhanyasi
description: Soulful audava raga derived from Todi; famous for Tyagaraja's 'Nagumomu'.

name: Hamsadhwani
description: Popular pentatonic raga; Dikshitar's 'Vatapi Ganapatim' is the iconic composition.

name: Kambhoji
description: Grand raga with majestic quality; used in many major compositions.

name: Kedaragowla
description: Vakra (zigzag) raga with a characteristic prayoga; deeply devotional.

name: Khamas
description: Raga with a distinctly romantic quality; often used in compositions addressed to Lord Vishnu.

name: Madhyamavathi
description: Powerful audava raga; Purandaradasa's 'Bhagya da Lakshmi Baramma' is iconic.

name: Malahari
description: Associated with dawn and auspicious beginnings; traditionally for Ganesha compositions.

name: Mohanam
description: Enchanting pentatonic raga (corresponds to Bhupali in Hindustani).

name: Nattai
description: Bold, vigorous raga; Tyagaraja's 'Jagadananda Karaka' (Pancharatna) is in Nata.

name: Punnagavarali
description: Intensely soulful raga; Dikshitar's 'Akshayalinga Vibho' is celebrated.

name: Revati
description: Simple, lyrical pentatonic raga with a devotional character.

name: Sahana
description: Deeply expressive vakra raga; Tyagaraja's 'Nidhi Chala Sukhama' is among its gems.

name: Saveri
description: Serene morning raga; corresponds to Asavari in Hindustani.

name: Sindhu Bhairavi
description: Emotionally charged raga used in light classical and film music context.

name: Sri
description: A grand raga of great antiquity; Tyagaraja's Pancharatna 'Endaro Mahanubhavulu' is in Sri.

name: Suddha Saveri
description: Bright, serene pentatonic raga with a morning quality.

name: Varamu
description: Devotional Todi-family raga used in many Annamacharya compositions.

name: Nattakurinji
description: Melodic raga of Shankarabharanam family; Tyagaraja's 'Etavunara' is iconic.

name: Amritavarshini
description: Rare raga traditionally believed to invoke rainfall; Dikshitar's 'Anandamritakarshini'.

name: Kapi
description: A vakra raga with a gentle, lyrical quality often used in padams and javalis.

name: Begada
description: Characteristic vakra prayogas give this raga a distinctive grandeur.

name: Arabhi
description: Grand audava raga; Tyagaraja's Pancharatna 'Sadhinchene' is in Arabhi.

name: Gowla
description: Traditional raga associated with morning practice; Tyagaraja's Pancharatna 'Dudukugala'.

name: Varali
description: Serious, introspective raga; Tyagaraja's Pancharatna 'Kana Kana Ruchira' is in Varali.
